subject: DevOps
title: Modules: Reusable Building Blocks
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Modules: Reusable Building Blocks.

hook.text: Every team writes the same VPC setup from scratch. Three teams, three slightly different versions. Bugs everywhere.

buildup.text: A Terraform module packages resources into a reusable unit. Call the module, pass variables, get infrastructure.

discovery.text: The platform team writes the VPC module once. Every team uses it. One source of truth, consistent results.

twist.text: Modules compose. A network module calls a subnet module which calls a security group module. Like building blocks.

climax.text: The Terraform Registry has thousands of public modules. Need a Kubernetes cluster? There's a verified module for it.

punchline.text: Modules are reusable IaC blocks. Write once, use everywhere.

quiz.question: What problem do Terraform modules solve?
options: They prevent teams from duplicating infrastructure code
